answer: The phrase 'six heroic enemies within' appears in the Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana by Mpu Dusun (Kawi language, Medieval era). The passage describes a steadfast practitioner warring against internal vices through austerities and vows. Full context: 'The steadfast one, seeking battle on the field of austerities and vows, warring against the six heroic enemies within, / His battle-array is the quality of truth in meditation; his chariot, the practice of compassion and more. / The sacred hand-gestures are his banner, the lion's roar his chanted spell, the supreme bow his inconceivable contemplation; / The highest arrow is enlightenment-wisdom, annihilating the foe, bearing the fruit of dharma-śūnyatā.' Work slug: unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a, Chapter 1.
